title: armeria alpina subsp. muelleri
intro: Armeria alpina subsp. muelleri, also known as the Alpine Marsh Bellflower, is a plant species that belongs to the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, offering potential health benefits including the reduction of swelling and improved cardiovascular health. Armeria alpina subsp. muelleri can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and natural remedies.
